raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":1.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The broad request to make the paper passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim.","R1.2":"Strengthening the passage requires checking whether the reasoning from prior work to hypothesis, method, results, and practical implication holds together.","R1.3":"The claims about benchmark superiority, ablation contribution, and practical utility must align with the reported datasets and results.","R1.4":"Because the artifact makes explicit strongest-published and improvement-over-prior-work claims, novelty positioning is a central responsibility.","R1.5":"Improving strength may require section- or paragraph-level reordering of background, gap, method, evidence, and contribution arc.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, force, concision, and academic register.","R1.7":"The passage relies heavily on named prior work and benchmark positioning, so citation coverage and attribution are central to making it stronger.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported factual claims and unverified benchmark history.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong claims such as strongest published method, so avoiding overclaim is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation rather than editing or evaluating the artifact itself.","RX.4":"Because the topic concerns detecting human versus synthetic respondents, the projection must account for possible misuse or harmful downstream effects.","RX.5":"The projection should track which responsibilities arise from specific features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The broad request to make the paper passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the passage requires checking whether the reasoning from prior work to hypothesis, method, results, and practical implication holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The claims about benchmark superiority, ablation contribution, and practical utility must align with the reported datasets and results.
rationale.R1.4: Because the artifact makes explicit strongest-published and improvement-over-prior-work claims, novelty positioning is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: Improving strength may require section- or paragraph-level reordering of background, gap, method, evidence, and contribution arc.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, force, concision, and academic register.
rationale.R1.7: The passage relies heavily on named prior work and benchmark positioning, so citation coverage and attribution are central to making it stronger.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported factual claims and unverified benchmark history.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims such as strongest published method, so avoiding overclaim is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation rather than editing or evaluating the artifact itself.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns detecting human versus synthetic respondents, the projection must account for possible misuse or harmful downstream effects.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should track which responsibilities arise from specific features of the delegation and artifact.
